topic.what: Comprehensive survey of TCM interventions targeting the Correa cascade — the stepwise progression from chronic atrophic gastritis through intestinal metaplasia and dysplasia to gastric adenocarcinoma — as a chemoprevention strategy.
topic.how: Systematic narrative review mapping TCM herbal formulas, single compounds (berberine, emodin, curcumin, resveratrol as TCM-derived or TCM-used agents), and integrative treatment protocols to specific cascade stages; mechanistic pathways (NF-κB, Wnt/β-catenin, PI3K/Akt, epigenetic modulation) reviewed alongside clinical evidence; frameworks for stage-matched TCM intervention proposed.
topic.why_matters: Gastric cancer remains a leading cause of cancer mortality globally; the Correa cascade offers defined intervention windows that TCM, with its multi-target pharmacology and long safety record in chronic use, is theoretically well-positioned to exploit — yet no unified evidence-based framework for TCM chemopreventive use across cascade stages existed prior to this review.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Comprehensiveness of TCM intervention evidence reviewed across all Correa cascade stages
characteristics.value: All five stages addressed: chronic active gastritis, chronic atrophic gastritis, intestinal metaplasia, dysplasia, early gastric cancer; strongest evidence at atrophic gastritis and intestinal metaplasia stages
characteristics.vs_baseline: Prior reviews focusing on single cascade stage
characteristics.evidence: Systematic literature synthesis across primary studies and meta-analyses
characteristics.context: Evidence quality diminishes at later cascade stages (dysplasia/early cancer) where ethical constraints limit TCM-only trial designs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Breadth of molecular pathways through which TCM exerts chemopreventive effects
characteristics.value: NF-κB, Wnt/β-catenin, PI3K/Akt/mTOR, epigenetic (DNMT, HDAC), and microRNA pathways reviewed; network pharmacology evidence cited
characteristics.evidence: Synthesis of in vitro, in vivo, and network pharmacology studies
characteristics.context: Multi-pathway coverage aligns with TCM multi-target philosophy but makes dose-response attribution complex

characteristics.what_it_measures: Overall quality of clinical evidence for TCM chemoprevention across the cascade
characteristics.value: Moderate — several RCTs and cohort studies at CAG and IM stages; limited high-quality evidence at dysplasia stage
characteristics.vs_baseline: Pharmaceutical chemoprevention evidence base (e.g., COX-2 inhibitors, aspirin)
characteristics.evidence: GRADE-style narrative appraisal of included clinical studies
characteristics.context: Strongest clinical evidence from Chinese single-centre RCTs; multi-centre international validation lacking

good_for: Researchers designing TCM chemoprevention trials across specific Correa cascade stages
Oncologists seeking evidence summary for integrative gastric cancer prevention
KB articles mapping TCM formulas to cancer prevention pathways
Combinatorial idea generation: crossing TCM targets with conventional chemoprevention agents
DOI: 10.1016/j.jep.2024.118812 for full framework and evidence tables
not_for: Established gastric cancer treatment (beyond prevention scope)
Patients requiring immediate H. pylori eradication therapy as primary intervention
requires: Familiarity with Correa cascade pathology and staging
Understanding of cancer chemoprevention trial design for evidence appraisal

novelty: First comprehensive framework explicitly mapping TCM interventions to each stage of the Correa cascade, enabling stage-matched treatment selection
Integrates network pharmacology multi-target data with clinical trial evidence to propose a mechanistically coherent chemopreventive rationale for TCM
Identifies intestinal metaplasia as the highest-leverage intervention window for TCM chemoprevention based on evidence density and reversibility biology
open_problems: No validated biomarkers for monitoring TCM chemopreventive response at the molecular level across cascade stages
Optimal duration of TCM chemoprevention and stopping criteria not established
Interaction between TCM chemopreventive agents and concurrent H. pylori eradication or proton pump inhibitor therapy not systematically reviewed
Long-term gastric cancer incidence data for TCM-treated populations absent from most reviewed studies
